markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Lizardfolk Lore](/Feats.aspx?ID=1050) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Lizardfolk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=226" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 58

</column>

---

You listened carefully to the tales passed down among your community. You gain the trained proficiency rank in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) and [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16). If you would automatically become trained in one of those skills (from your background or class, for example), you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You also become trained in Iruxi [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8).
name: Lizardfolk Lore

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Marsh Runner](/Feats.aspx?ID=1051) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Lizardfolk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=226" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 58

**Prerequisites**
You have a swim speed

</column>

---

You are adept at moving through marshy terrain. When you use the Step action, you can ignore difficult terrain caused by flooding, swamps, or quicksand. In addition, when you use the [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) skill to Balance on narrow surfaces or uneven marshy ground, you aren’t flat-footed, and if you roll a success on the Acrobatics check, you get a critical success instead.
name: Marsh Runner

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Parthenogenic Hatchling](/Feats.aspx?ID=1052) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Lizardfolk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=226" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 58

</column>

---

You were hatched from an unfertilized egg during hard times for your people, and you are a biological copy of your mother. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against diseases. Each of your successful saving throws against a disease reduces its stage by 2, or by 1 for a virulent disease. Each critical success against an ongoing disease reduces its stage by 3, or by 2 for a virulent disease. You take damage only every 2 hours from thirst and every 2 days from starvation, rather than every hour and every day.

**Special** You can take this feat only at 1st level.
name: Parthenogenic Hatchling

You can shed your tail to escape. You cease being grabbed, then Stride without triggering any reactions from the creature that grabbed you. It takes 1 week for your tail to fully grow back. markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Backup Disguise](/Feats.aspx?ID=1064) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 71

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5)

**Access**
members of the Firebrands gain access to them once they reach the rank of second mark

</column>

---

You have a specific disguise that you keep at the ready, worn underneath your outer garment. You can change into this disguise to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) as a 3-action activity. If you have master proficiency in Deception, it is instead a 2-action activity, and if you have legendary proficiency, it is a single action. You can create a new backup disguise by spending the normal amount of time it takes you to Impersonate, but you can have only one backup disguise at a time. Having a backup disguise doesn’t allow you to remove your armor or any other complex piece of clothing any more quickly, but once you have those off, the disguise is readily available. Because you have the backup disguise at the ready, it’s possible that a thorough search might reveal some elements of the disguise (see [Conceal an Object](/Actions.aspx?ID=61) in the [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) skill).

<title level="2">Firebrand Options</title>

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 71

The Firebrands use a variety of unorthodox equipment and techniques to achieve their goals, whether those goals include drawing attention, or subterfuge and subtlety. The following Firebrand options are uncommon, and members of the Firebrands gain access to them once they reach the rank of second mark. The [Entourage](/Feats.aspx?ID=1066) feat is rare even for members of the Firebrands, as it requires a certain degree of fame and in-world activity to acquire, and potentially requires the player or GM to keep track of additional background NPCs. Typically, only third mark and fourth mark Firebrands have the notoriety and social influence required to maintain an entourage. While Firebrands seek to build an entourage more than most adventurers, it’s not unreasonable for any famous character to gain access to it if the players are interested in playing with the [Entourage](/Feats.aspx?ID=1066) feat.

**Related Feats**: [Entourage](/Feats.aspx?ID=1066), [Sow Rumor](/Feats.aspx?ID=1065)
name: Backup Disguise

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 71 **Prerequisites** Expert in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) **Access** members of the Firebrands gain access to them once they reach the rank of second mark --- You spread rumors, which may or may not be true, about a specific subject. If the subject of your rumor is not currently the subject of any contradictory rumors, this takes as long as it would normally take you to [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=49) (typically 2 hours), at the end of which the GM rolls a secret Deception check to see how well you spread the rumor. If your rumor matches any current rumors about the subject, it takes less time to spread the rumor, and if you are attempting to overtake a particularly popular and contradictory rumor, it takes much longer or may be impossible. The DC similarly increases or decreases depending on how plausible your rumor is. **Critical Success** Your rumor spreads like wildfire. Anyone who succeeds at a check to Gather Information on the specific subject learns your rumor in preference to other rumors about the subject. Your rumor persists for 1 month.
**Success** You successfully spread the rumor. Anyone who succeeds at a check to Gather Information on the specific subject adds your rumor to the list of rumors they could learn about the subject. Your rumor persists for 1 week.
**Failure** Your rumor dies off, never becoming popular enough for other people to learn it via Gather Information.
**Critical Failure** You are unable to spread a rumor and take a –4 circumstance penalty to Deception checks to Sow Rumors about the same subject within the same region for 1 week. In addition, a rumor spreads about someone trying to spread false rumors about the subject. Firebrand Options **Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 71 The Firebrands use a variety of unorthodox equipment and techniques to achieve their goals, whether those goals include drawing attention, or subterfuge and subtlety. The following Firebrand options are uncommon, and members of the Firebrands gain access to them once they reach the rank of second mark. The [Entourage](/Feats.aspx?ID=1066) feat is rare even for members of the Firebrands, as it requires a certain degree of fame and in-world activity to acquire, and potentially requires the player or GM to keep track of additional background NPCs. Typically, only third mark and fourth mark Firebrands have the notoriety and social influence required to maintain an entourage. While Firebrands seek to build an entourage more than most adventurers, it’s not unreasonable for any famous character to gain access to it if the players are interested in playing with the [Entourage](/Feats.aspx?ID=1066) feat. **Related Feats**: [Backup Disguise](/Feats.aspx?ID=1064), [Entourage](/Feats.aspx?ID=1066)
name: Sow Rumor

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Entourage](/Feats.aspx?ID=1066) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 72

**Prerequisites**
Master in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6); [Hobnobber](/Feats.aspx?ID=791)

</column>

---

You have a small group of admirers who tend to follow you around while you’re in civilized settlements. Your admirers are helpful to you, allowing you to make simple [Requests](/Actions.aspx?ID=51) of them such as purchasing basic equipment with your funds or finding a room at an inn. These admirers do not travel with you to places that are obviously dangerous, including most adventure locations, but they’ll wait for you at the nearest settlement. Your admirers never use actions in encounter mode other than to retreat, and they abandon you if you intentionally bring them harm or at the GM’s discretion. When you [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=49), you can use your admirers to assist you, granting a +1 circumstance bonus to your Diplomacy check and reducing the time it takes even further (typically to 30 minutes, rather than 1 hour with the Hobnobber feat alone). If you have the [Sow Rumor](/Feats.aspx?ID=1065) feat, you can have your admirers assist you, with the same benefits as Gather Information.

<title level="2">Firebrand Options</title>

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 71

The Firebrands use a variety of unorthodox equipment and techniques to achieve their goals, whether those goals include drawing attention, or subterfuge and subtlety. The following Firebrand options are uncommon, and members of the Firebrands gain access to them once they reach the rank of second mark. The [Entourage](/Feats.aspx?ID=1066) feat is rare even for members of the Firebrands, as it requires a certain degree of fame and in-world activity to acquire, and potentially requires the player or GM to keep track of additional background NPCs. Typically, only third mark and fourth mark Firebrands have the notoriety and social influence required to maintain an entourage. While Firebrands seek to build an entourage more than most adventurers, it’s not unreasonable for any famous character to gain access to it if the players are interested in playing with the [Entourage](/Feats.aspx?ID=1066) feat.

**Related Feats**: [Backup Disguise](/Feats.aspx?ID=1064), [Sow Rumor](/Feats.aspx?ID=1065)

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Firebrand Braggart Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1067) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
<trait label="Misfortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=110" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 74

**Archetype** [Firebrand Braggart](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=24)

**Prerequisites**
Charisma 14; second mark member of the Firebrands

</column>

---

Your Firebrand training has taught you that achieving something is more satisfying if you boast about it first. You declare a boast about a particular skill action, such as [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=28), [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53), or [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26). The first time you attempt this action within the next minute in a context the GM deems both suitably challenging and meaningful, you must roll twice and use the lower result. If you succeed at this check, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to checks to attempt the same action for 10 minutes. If you fail this check or don’t attempt the action within 1 minute, you instead take a –1 circumstance penalty on checks to attempt the same action for 1 hour.

 If the chosen action can be used with multiple skills, such as [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=24) or Recall Knowledge, you must specify which skill you are using for that particular action, such as using [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) to Identify Magic, and your bonus or penalty after the boast applies only to checks using that skill for that action. Once you declare a boast about a particular action, you cannot declare a boast about the same action until the next time you make daily preparations, regardless of whether you succeed or fail at the check.

 Typically a challenging task is one with at least a standard DC for your level, though the difficulty may be higher depending on the situation. A meaningful context is one where the action’s success or failure is relevant to the pursuit of your goals, rather than a boast you made simply to gain a bonus later.

**Special** You can’t select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the Firebrand Braggart archetype.

<title level="2">Firebrand Braggart Dedication leads to...</title>
[Boaster's Challenge](/Feats.aspx?ID=1068), [Bravo's Determination](/Feats.aspx?ID=1070), [Daring Act](/Feats.aspx?ID=1069), [Great Boaster](/Feats.aspx?ID=1071)
name: Firebrand Braggart Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: Charisma 14; second mark member of the Firebrands

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Boaster's Challenge](/Feats.aspx?ID=1068) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 74

**Archetype** [Firebrand Braggart](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=24)

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5), Expert in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6), or Expert in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7); [Firebrand Braggart Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1067)

</column>

---

You call out a foe, causing them to become flustered and easier to defeat. Select one creature you can see and declare a challenge. For your challenge, attempt a Deception, Diplomacy, or Intimidation check against that creature’s Will DC. Your challenge must include a [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101), [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), or [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) component and gains the corresponding trait. If you succeed, you gain a +2 status bonus to damage rolls with your weapon or unarmed Strikes against that foe until the end of your next turn; if you have master proficiency in the skill you used, you gain a +4 status bonus instead, and if you’re legendary, you gain a +6 status bonus. On a critical success, the status bonus lasts for 3 rounds instead. Whether you succeed or fail, creatures who witnessed your challenge gain a +4 circumstance bonus to their Will DCs against your attempts to challenge for 1 minute.

<title level="2">Boaster's Challenge leads to...</title>
[Demanding Challenge](/Feats.aspx?ID=1073)
name: Boaster's Challenge
prerequisite_markdown: Expert in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5), Expert in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6), or Expert in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7); [Firebrand Braggart Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1067)

[Daring Act](/Feats.aspx?ID=1069) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>

</column>

---

You attempt a death-defying maneuver to distract your enemies. Select one foe within reach and attempt either an Acrobatics or Athletics check against your target’s Reflex DC. If you succeed, you can Stride up to half your Speed (or up to your full Speed if you critically succeed) without triggering reactions from the target due to your movement, and the target is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against the next melee attack you attempt against it before the end of your turn.

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 75 **Archetype** [Firebrand Braggart](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=24) **Prerequisites** Expert in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5); [Firebrand Braggart Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1067) **Trigger** A foe’s Strike reduces you to 0 Hit Points; you were not at 1 Hit Point and you would not be killed. --- Your confidence borders on self-delusion, but it keeps you going against overwhelming odds. Attempt a Deception check against the attack DC of the triggering Strike. You take a penalty to this check equal to twice your wounded value, if any. If the triggering attack was a critical hit, use the result one degree of success worse than what you rolled. **Critical Success** You avoid being knocked out and remain at 1 Hit Point.
**Success** You avoid being knocked out and remain at 1 Hit Point, but you increase your [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=42) value by 1.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Great Boaster](/Feats.aspx?ID=1071) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 75

**Archetype** [Firebrand Braggart](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=24)

**Prerequisites**
Charisma 16; [Firebrand Braggart Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1067)

</column>

---

Your bragging is particularly effective. If you succeed at a boast, you can attempt a great boast about that same skill by using your Firebrand Braggart Dedication action about the same action during the duration of your original boast’s circumstance bonus; this is an exception to the rule that you can’t make another boast about the same action until your next daily preparations. A great boast uses the Firebrand Braggart Dedication action, except that if you succeed, your circumstance bonus increases to +2 and remains for 1 hour. If you fail the great boast, you take a –2 circumstance penalty to attempt the same action for 4 hours. Once you declare a great boast about a particular action, you can’t declare a great boast about the same action until the next time you make daily preparations, regardless of whether you succeed or fail at the check. The minimum challenging task for a great boast is one with at least a hard DC for your level.
name: Great Boaster

If you critically succeed at your Daring Act, you can enter the target’s space and remain there until the beginning of your next turn or until the target moves, whichever comes first. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to AC, and your target is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against your attacks while you are sharing its space in this way. If any creature other than your target hits you with an attack roll while you are sharing your target’s space, attempt a DC 15 flat check. On a success, resolve the attack against the target instead of you, using the same attack roll result that hit you.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />

</column>

---

You have forged your body to comply with the commands of your mind—orders steeped in the Measure and the Chain. You gain expert proficiency in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) (or in another skill in which you’re trained of your choice, if you were already an expert in Intimidation). You gain the armor specialization effects of Hellknight plate, and your resistance from that armor specialization is 1 higher than normal. In addition, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Intimidation checks while wearing your Hellknight plate.

**Special** You cannot select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the Hellknight or [Hellknight armiger](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=20) archetypes.

prerequisite_markdown: [Hellknight Armiger Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=907); lawful alignment; member of a Hellknight Order; passed the Hellknight Test

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 84 **Archetype** [Hellknight](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=25) **Prerequisites** [Hellknight Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1078) --- You sense chaos as an unsettling feeling of nervous energy. When in the presence of an aura of chaos that is powerful or overwhelming, you eventually detect the aura, though you might not do so instantly and you can’t pinpoint the location. This is a vague sense, similar to humans’ sense of smell. A chaotic creature using a disguise or otherwise trying to hide its presence attempts a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) check against your Perception DC to hide its aura from you. If the creature succeeds at its Deception check, it is temporarily immune to your Sense Chaos for 1 day. Alignment Aura The strength of an alignment aura depends on the level of the creature, item, or spell. The auras of undead, clerics and other divine spellcasters with a patron deity, and creatures from the Outer Sphere are one step stronger than normal (faint instead of none, for example). ## Alignment Aura
**Creature or Item Level****Spell or Effect Level****Aura Strength**
0-5—None
6-100-3Faint
11-154-7Moderate
16-208-9Powerful
21+10Overwhelming
name: Sense Chaos

You’ve trained with your order’s weapon and your Hellknight plate long enough to apply your expertise from other weapons and armor. If you have expert proficiency in any weapon or unarmed attack, you gain expert proficiency in your order’s weapon as well. If you have expert proficiency in any armor or unarmored defense, you gain expert proficiency in Hellknight plate as well.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Hellknight Signifer Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1082) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 85

**Archetype** [Hellknight Signifer](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=26)

**Prerequisites**
spellcasting class feature; [Hellknight Armiger Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=907); lawful alignment; member of a Hellknight Order; passed the Hellknight Test

</column>

---

You have bolstered your force of will with the power of the Measure and the Chain. Upon initiation, you receive a signifer mask, often devoid of eyeholes or other decorative features. The mask doesn’t obscure your vision, though it makes it impossible for others to see your eyes. While wearing your signifer’s mask, you gain +1 circumstance bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to Lie, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks, and Deception DCs against [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=85). You gain expert proficiency in Intimidation (or in another skill in which you’re trained of your choice, if you were already an expert in Intimidation) as well as in your choice of [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13).

**Special** You cannot select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the Hellknight signifer or [Hellknight armiger](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=20) archetypes.

<title level="2">Hellknight Signifer Dedication leads to...</title>
[Advanced Order Training](/Feats.aspx?ID=1076), [Gaze of Veracity](/Feats.aspx?ID=1084), [Hellknight Order Cross-Training](/Feats.aspx?ID=1077), [Masked Casting](/Feats.aspx?ID=1083), [Signifer Armor Expertise](/Feats.aspx?ID=1086), [Signifer's Sight](/Feats.aspx?ID=1085)
name: Hellknight Signifer Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: spellcasting class feature; [Hellknight Armiger Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=907); lawful alignment; member of a Hellknight Order; passed the Hellknight Test

The magic flowing through your signifer’s mask allows you to see in the dark, and the mask dampens the distraction caused by sudden flashes of bright light. While wearing your signifer’s mask, you gain [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415). Additionally, if you are [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) while wearing your signifer’s mask and the target is concealed only because you are dazzled, you reduce the flat check DC to target that creature from 5 to 3.
name: Signifer's Sight

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Champion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=24" />
<trait label="Fighter" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=71" />

**Access**
A character who is a member of the Knights of Lastwall has access to this option.

**Requirements**
You are wielding a shield.

</column>

---

You brace your shield with both hands, enhancing its potential for both offense and defense. When in this stance, you wield the shield with both hands. When wielding a shield this way, increase the weapon damage die of the shield’s boss or spikes by one step, and increase the shield’s Hardness by 2 when using the [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839) reaction.

<title level="2">Everstand Stance leads to...</title>
[Everstand Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=1088)

<title level="2">Knights of Lastwall Class Feats</title>

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 90

The following class feats are available to Knights of Lastwall. Those with the champion trait are champion class feats. Those with both the champion and fighter traits are both champion class feats and fighter class feats; when you take one of these feats, it loses the trait that doesn’t apply to your class.

**Related Feats**: [Everstand Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=1088), [Impassable Wall Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=1091), [Light of Revelation](/Feats.aspx?ID=1089), [Sun Blade](/Feats.aspx?ID=1090)
name: Everstand Stance
requirement_markdown: You are wielding a shield.

When you are between an ally and a foe and would provide lesser cover to your ally against the foe’s attacks, you twist to become even more obtrusive, providing standard cover instead.

    level="1"

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Unshakable Idealism](/Feats.aspx?ID=1093) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 94

**Archetype** [Knight Vigilant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=27)

**Prerequisites**
[Knight Vigilant](/Feats.aspx?ID=1092); expert in Will saves

</column>

---

Your faith and hope can’t be shaken, and you won’t flee when lives are at stake. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus on saves against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) effects and [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) effects that inflict despair. When you would gain the [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=17) condition, you can choose to stand your ground and be [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) for the effect’s duration as well, preventing you from running and allowing you to re-enter the fight more quickly when the effect ends.
name: Unshakable Idealism

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Endure Death's Touch](/Feats.aspx?ID=1094) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 94

**Archetype** [Knight Vigilant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=27)

**Prerequisites**
[Knight Vigilant](/Feats.aspx?ID=1092); expert in medium or heavy armor

**Trigger**
An undead hits you with an unarmed attack.

**Requirements**
You are wearing medium or heavy armor in which you have expert proficiency.

</column>

---

Your conviction redirects the undead’s attack through your armor and then harmlessly away. You gain resistance 5 to physical damage and resistance 10 to negative damage against the triggering attack. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus on any saving throws against effects from the undead’s natural attack; if you succeed at the saving throw, you get a critical success instead.
name: Endure Death's Touch
prerequisite_markdown: [Knight Vigilant](/Feats.aspx?ID=1092); expert in medium or heavy armor
requirement_markdown: You are wearing medium or heavy armor in which you have expert proficiency.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Knight Reclaimant Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1097) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 95

**Archetype** [Knight Reclaimant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=28)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15); Trained in [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16); any non-evil alignment; member of the Knights of Lastwall of knight rank

**Access**
A character who is a member of the Knights of Lastwall has access to this option.

</column>

---

You have sworn the Crimson Oath and become a full member of the Crimson Reclaimers. You gain expert proficiency in Stealth and Survival. Whenever you roll a success on a saving throw against an undead’s special ability, you get a critical success instead. This applies to any abilities that come from being undead, including an undead’s innate spells (but not its prepared or spontaneous spells).

**Special** You cannot select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the Knight Reclaimant archetype.

<title level="2">Knight Reclaimant Dedication leads to...</title>
[Ashen Veil](/Feats.aspx?ID=3589), [Blade of the Crimson Oath](/Feats.aspx?ID=1101), [Crimson Oath Devotion](/Feats.aspx?ID=3595), [Cut them Down, Burn them Out](/Feats.aspx?ID=7748), [For Love, For Lightning](/Feats.aspx?ID=3592), [Hilt Hammer](/Feats.aspx?ID=3591), [Invoke the Crimson Oath](/Feats.aspx?ID=1098), [Reaper of Repose](/Feats.aspx?ID=1100), [Reclaimant Plea](/Feats.aspx?ID=3593), [Shall Not Falter, Shall Not Rout](/Feats.aspx?ID=2168), [Shepherd of Desolation](/Feats.aspx?ID=3594), [Survivor of Desolation](/Feats.aspx?ID=1099), [The Tyrant Falls!](/Feats.aspx?ID=3596), [Voice Cold as Death](/Feats.aspx?ID=3590)
name: Knight Reclaimant Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: Trained in [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15); Trained in [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16); any non-evil alignment; member of the Knights of Lastwall of knight rank

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Invoke the Crimson Oath](/Feats.aspx?ID=1098) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 95

**Archetype** [Knight Reclaimant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=28)

**Prerequisites**
[Knight Reclaimant Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1097)

</column>

---

You can invoke the Crimson Oath to unleash blasts of ruby energy from your weapon. You gain the [_invoke the Crimson Oath_](/Spells.aspx?ID=556) focus spell. If you don’t already have one, you gain a focus pool of 1 Focus Point, which you can regain using the Refocus activity to recite the Crimson Oath and meditate on its teachings; if you already have a focus pool, increase the number of Focus Points in your focus pool by 1. Your knight reclaimant focus spells are divine spells; when you gain this feat, you become trained in divine spell attacks and spell DCs. Your key spellcasting ability for these spells is Charisma.

<title level="2">Invoke the Crimson Oath leads to...</title>
[The Tyrant Falls!](/Feats.aspx?ID=3596)
name: Invoke the Crimson Oath

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Survivor of Desolation](/Feats.aspx?ID=1099) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 95

**Archetype** [Knight Reclaimant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=28)

**Prerequisites**
[Knight Reclaimant Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1097)

</column>

---

You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to saving throws against unnatural weather events or environmental hazards occurring in blighted or otherwise unnaturally marred regions. If you roll a success on a saving throw against such an effect, you get a critical success instead; if you roll a critical failure, you get a failure instead. This does not apply to unnatural events created or directed by active effects, such as spells that create anomalous weather or hazardous areas.

<title level="2">Survivor of Desolation leads to...</title>
[Shepherd of Desolation](/Feats.aspx?ID=3594)
name: Survivor of Desolation

[Reaper of Repose](/Feats.aspx?ID=1100) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

Your skill and fervor allow you to pierce undead defenses and exploit their weaknesses. When you make an attack using a weapon with which you have master proficiency and hit an undead creature, you ignore 5 points of the creature’s resistances, and the undead’s weaknesses are 2 higher against your attack.

<title level="2">Reaper of Repose leads to...</title>
[Grave Threat](/Feats.aspx?ID=7747)

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Magaambyan Attendant Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1102) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 101

**Archetype** [Magaambyan Attendant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=29)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) or Trained in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10); member of the Magaambya of attendant rank

**Access**
A character who is Magaambyan of at least attendant rank has access to this option.

</column>

---

You devote much of your study to halcyon magic. You gain the ability to cast a single [arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1) or [primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) cantrip of your choice, (as is normal for cantrips, it is heightened to a spell level equal to half your level, rounded up). If you weren’t already, you become trained in that tradition’s spell DCs and spell attack rolls, with Intelligence as your spellcasting ability if you choose arcane or Wisdom as your spellcasting ability if you choose primal.

 Regardless of whether you choose an arcane or primal cantrip, you also either become trained in Arcana or Nature, or an expert in one of those skills in which you were already trained.

 When you gain this feat, choose to affiliate with the Cascade Bearers, Emerald Boughs, Rain–Scribes, Tempest-Sun Mages, or Uzunjati. This grants you additional feats available to only that branch.

**Special** You cannot select another dedication feat other than [Halcyon Speaker Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1115) until you have gained two other feats from the Magaambyan Attendant or [halcyon speaker](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=30) archetype.

<title level="2">Magaambyan Attendant Dedication leads to...</title>
[Cascade Bearer's Flexibility](/Feats.aspx?ID=1105), [Cascade Bearer's Spellcasting](/Feats.aspx?ID=1110), [Emerald Boughs Accustomation](/Feats.aspx?ID=1106), [Emerald Boughs Hideaway](/Feats.aspx?ID=1111), [Faith in the Fallen](/Feats.aspx?ID=7759), [Halcyon Speaker Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1115), [Mask Familiar](/Feats.aspx?ID=1103), [Rain-Scribe Mobility](/Feats.aspx?ID=1112), [Rain-Scribe Sustenance](/Feats.aspx?ID=1107), [Tempest-Sun Redirection](/Feats.aspx?ID=1108), [Uzunjati Storytelling](/Feats.aspx?ID=1109)
name: Magaambyan Attendant Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: Trained in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) or Trained in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10); member of the Magaambya of attendant rank

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mask Familiar](/Feats.aspx?ID=1103) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 101

**Archetype** [Magaambyan Attendant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=29)

**Prerequisites**
[Magaambyan Attendant Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1102)

</column>

---

Your mask takes on a personality of its own, allowing it to serve as your [familiar](Familiars.aspx). Though you can still wear it as a mask, you can detach it from your face to grant it a bodily form of colored light that allows it to move around; usually, this is a miniature form of the animal the mask represents. Detaching or reattaching the familiar to transform it is a two-action activity. Even in mask form, the familiar can move around on its master’s face and speaks in a distinct voice (if it can talk). It is easily recognized as more than a simple mask unless the familiar succeeds at a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) check to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) a mask. Other than its appearance and the fact that it can be worn, it functions as other familiars. Your mask familiar has access to the mask freeze familiar ability

**Mask Freeze**: When in mask form, your familiar can hide its obvious supernatural qualities to pass as a simple, unassuming mask. It doesn’t need to Impersonate to fool a passing glance, and it gains a +4 circumstance bonus to its Deception DC against an active observer [Seeking](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) or otherwise studying it.

<title level="2">Mask Familiar leads to...</title>
[Adaptive Mask Familiar](/Feats.aspx?ID=1104), [Crusader's Masquerade](/Feats.aspx?ID=7758)
name: Mask Familiar

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Emerald Boughs Accustomation](/Feats.aspx?ID=1106) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 102

**Archetype** [Magaambyan Attendant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=29)

**Prerequisites**
[Magaambyan Attendant Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1102); Expert in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14); Emerald Boughs affiliation

</column>

---

You are comfortable and socially capable in almost any cultural context. When you use the Society skill to [Subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=27), if you roll a critical failure, you get a failure instead; if you roll a success, you get a critical success instead; and if you roll a critical success, you can provide for another additional creature. Furthermore, when you attempt a Society check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) about cultural practices and roll a critical failure, you get a failure instead.
name: Emerald Boughs Accustomation
prerequisite_markdown: [Magaambyan Attendant Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1102); Expert in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14); Emerald Boughs affiliation

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rain-Scribe Sustenance](/Feats.aspx?ID=1107) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 102

**Archetype** [Magaambyan Attendant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=29)

**Prerequisites**
[Magaambyan Attendant Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1102); Rain-Scribes affiliation

</column>

---

You use the power of your Rain-Scribe magic to draw on a stream of primal power for sustenance. You don’t need to eat or drink and need only 6 hours of rest to feel well rested. You must still rest at least 8 hours and spend 1 hour of preparation to regain resources usable only a limited number of times per day, such as spell slots.

 You can draw on primal power to stay awake and alert while otherwise resting your body and mind, gaining the full benefits of an 8-hour rest without falling asleep. When you do, you lose the other benefits of Rain- Scribe Sustenance for 1 week while your primal energies recharge.
name: Rain-Scribe Sustenance

    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tempest-Sun Redirection](/Feats.aspx?ID=1108) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

</column>

---

If the next action you use is to Cast a Spell from your spell slots that damages other creatures, you can bend some of that spell’s offensive energy, protecting allies in the area and boosting your own defenses against certain foes. Select any number of targets of the spell and reduce the spell’s damage to those targets by an amount equal to the spell’s level. Until your next turn, when one of those targets damages you, that damage is reduced by twice the spell’s level.

<title level="2">Tempest-Sun Redirection leads to...</title>
[Tempest-Sun Shielding](/Feats.aspx?ID=1113)
name: Tempest-Sun Redirection

    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Uzunjati Storytelling](/Feats.aspx?ID=1109) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 102

**Archetype** [Magaambyan Attendant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=29)

**Prerequisites**
[Magaambyan Attendant Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1102); Uzunjati affiliation; master in a [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) skill

</column>

---

You tell a quick story or begin telling a longer tale. This is most useful when you want to prove your scholarly credibility or impress someone quickly. Roll a check with a skill that can be used to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) in which you have expert proficiency, using the result entry for the [Perform](/Actions.aspx?ID=59) action. Like Performing, Storytelling rarely has an effect on its own, but it might influence the DCs of subsequent Diplomacy checks against the observers—or even change their attitudes—if the GM sees fit. This action is not a Performance and cannot be used in place of anything that requires you to Perform.

<title level="2">Uzunjati Storytelling leads to...</title>
[Adept Storyteller](/Feats.aspx?ID=7756), [Cautious Word](/Feats.aspx?ID=7757), [Uzunjati Recollection](/Feats.aspx?ID=1114)
name: Uzunjati Storytelling
prerequisite_markdown: [Magaambyan Attendant Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1102); Uzunjati affiliation; master in a [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) skill

Your Cascade Bearer training allows you to broaden the horizons of your halcyon spells further than most Magaambyans, weaving in spiritual magic. You can select halcyon spells from the [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) or [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) spell lists in additional to the arcane or primal spell lists, though they are still halcyon spells (so you choose when casting if they are arcane or primal spells). You gain a halcyon cantrip and a halcyon 1st-level spell.

<title level="2">Cascade Bearer's Spellcasting leads to...</title>
[Shattered Sacrament](/Feats.aspx?ID=7742)
name: Cascade Bearer's Spellcasting

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Emerald Boughs Hideaway](/Feats.aspx?ID=1111) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 103

**Archetype** [Magaambyan Attendant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=29)

**Prerequisites**
[Magaambyan Attendant Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1102); Emerald Boughs affiliation

</column>

---

You use Emerald Bough techniques to create a small extradimensional space called a hideaway that lasts as long as you are conscious. The hideaway functions as a [_type I bag of holding_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=249), but has no Bulk and takes no hands; it holds one object with a maximum Bulk equal to your highest mental ability score modifier; as you open the hideaway with pure thought, activating it to retrieve an item is a free action with the envision component instead of a single action with the Interact component. The hideaway’s access point appears as an obviously magical gap within 1 foot of your body. Only you can access the hideaway; you can you use an Interact action each round to physically hold it open for another person. A successful [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) spell or similar effect against your spell DC and the hideaway’s counteract level of 2 expels the object; the object is also expelled if you become unconscious. After a successful _dispel magic _spell or when you become conscious again, the hideaway immediately reforms with no additional action from you. You can also dismiss the hideaway (if it is empty) or re-form it using a single action with the envision component.
name: Emerald Boughs Hideaway

You surround yourself in a mantle of Rain-Scribe magic and Stride twice; difficult terrain does not reduce your Speed, even if the difficult terrain has been manipulated by magic. Your magic clears the difficult terrain in each square you entered; until your next turn, those squares aren’t difficult terrain for any creature walking through them (or are normal difficult terrain if they were previously greater difficult terrain).

A character who is Magaambyan of at least attendant rank has access to this option.

</column>

---

You devote much of your study to halcyon magic. You gain two common Halcyon cantrips and two common 1st-level halcyon spells (see [Halcyon Spells](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=30)). In addition to being able to cast your halcyon spells via your arcane or primal spell slots, you also gain a 1st-level halcyon spell slot. You can use your halcyon spell slots to spontaneously cast your halcyon spells.

**Special** You cannot select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the halcyon speaker archetype.

<title level="2">Halcyon Speaker Dedication leads to...</title>
[Dualistic Synergy](/Feats.aspx?ID=1116), [Halcyon Spellcasting Initiate](/Feats.aspx?ID=1118), [Persistent Creation](/Feats.aspx?ID=1117), [Shattered Sacrament](/Feats.aspx?ID=7742), [Speak for the Gravelands](/Feats.aspx?ID=7743)

If the spell is arcane, you can attempt a skill check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) with a +1 status bonus to the check after casting the spell, or a +2 status bonus if the spell is 7th level or higher. You gain one more common halcyon spell of each level of halcyon spell you can cast other than your highest (you do not gain an additional cantrip). You refine your halcyon magic. You gain two common 4th-level halcyon spells and two 5th-level halcyon spells. You also gain a 4th-level halcyon spell slot and a 5th-level halcyon spell slot. Your proficiency rank in either arcane or primal spell DCs and spell attack rolls advances from trained to expert.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Fulminating Synergy](/Feats.aspx?ID=1123) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 105

**Archetype** [Halcyon Speaker](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=30)

**Prerequisites**
[Dualistic Synergy](/Feats.aspx?ID=1116)

</column>

---

When you draw upon your spells’ synergy, you create a physical manifestation of their magical resonance and invoke it to harm a foe. When you use Dualistic Synergy, choose a damage type: acid, cold, electricity, or fire. In addition to Dualistic Synergy’s normal effect, a foe within 30 feet of you takes an amount of damage of the chosen type equal to the spell’s level. If the spell already deals damage of the chosen type, combine the damage before applying weaknesses and resistances.

<title level="2">Fulminating Synergy leads to...</title>
[Synergistic Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=1125)
name: Fulminating Synergy

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Synergistic Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=1125) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Metamagic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=107" />
</traits>

</column>

---

You combine your synergy and spells into a fantastic web of magic, allowing you to cast two spells at once. If the next action you use is to Cast a Spell from one of your halcyon spell slots, after that spell resolves, you can Cast another Spell as a free action. This spell must be cast from one of your halcyon spell slots and be a spell that would normally take two actions to cast.
name: Synergistic Spell
prerequisite_markdown: [Halcyon Spellcasting Initiate](/Feats.aspx?ID=1118); [Fulminating Synergy](/Feats.aspx?ID=1123); Legendary in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2); Legendary in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)

---

You intentionally set off the trap, but you take special precautions to protect yourself and your allies from the trap’s effects. For the next 2 rounds, if the trap rolls a critical success on an attack roll, it gets a normal success instead, and if anyone rolls a critical failure on their saving throw against the trap, they get a normal failure instead.

If you have the Battle Assessment feat, you gain the effects of Educated Assessment whenever you Recall Knowledge (such as with the [Automatic Knowledge](/Feats.aspx?ID=757) feat).

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Scrollmaster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1130) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 113

**Archetype** [Scrollmaster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=31)

**Prerequisites**
expert in any [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skill; member of the Pathfinder Society affiliated with the School of Scrolls

**Access**
A character who is a member of the Pathfinder Society has access to this option.

</column>

---

Your experience chronicling adventures and discoveries grants you heightened discernment and memory. For 24 hours after learning a prominent fact—such as the name of an NPC you have met, the details of your mission briefing, and similar information—you can recall it without attempting a check, though this doesn’t allow you to automatically memorize long strings of numbers or text. You also gain a +2 circumstance bonus to checks to remember a detail that has come up before in the current adventure, even if it was more than 24 hours ago.

 If you have the [Thorough Reports](/Feats.aspx?ID=874) feat and have expert proficiency in the skill you’re using to Recall Knowledge, your circumstance bonus to Recall Knowledge about creatures from that feat increases to +4.

**Special** You cannot select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the Scrollmaster archetype.

<title level="2">Scrollmaster Dedication leads to...</title>
[Bestiary Scholar](/Feats.aspx?ID=2236), [Cautious Delver](/Feats.aspx?ID=2237), [Font of Knowledge](/Feats.aspx?ID=1133), [Foolproof Instructions](/Feats.aspx?ID=2232), [Innate Magic Intuition](/Feats.aspx?ID=2233), [Lore Seeker](/Feats.aspx?ID=1131), [Magic Finder](/Feats.aspx?ID=2234), [Masterful Obfuscation](/Feats.aspx?ID=2238), [Transcribe Moment](/Feats.aspx?ID=2235), [Unravel Mysteries](/Feats.aspx?ID=1132), [Ward Mind](/Feats.aspx?ID=2240)
name: Scrollmaster Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: expert in any [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skill; member of the Pathfinder Society affiliated with the School of Scrolls

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Lore Seeker](/Feats.aspx?ID=1131) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Prerequisites**
[Scrollmaster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1130)

</column>

---

You see what others don’t, and you’ve developed magical tricks to find hidden truth. You can cast [_comprehend language_](/Spells.aspx?ID=46), [_restore senses_](/Spells.aspx?ID=259), and [_see invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=271) as innate spells, each once per day. If you could already cast spells, these spells are of the same tradition. Otherwise, they are arcane spells, you use Intelligence as your spellcasting ability, and you become trained in spell attack rolls and spell DCs for arcane spells.

<title level="2">Lore Seeker leads to...</title>
[Determined Lore Seeker](/Feats.aspx?ID=2239)
name: Lore Seeker

<traits>

<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />

**Prerequisites**
master in a [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=22) skill; [Scrollmaster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1130)

</column>

---

You can understand the true meaning of texts quickly, and even when you get lost or hit a dead end, you aren’t discouraged from making further attempts to get to the bottom of things. When you Decipher Writing, you need only half as long as usual (reducing the time from about 1 minute to about half a minute per page), and if you fail, you don’t take the usual –2 circumstance penalty to further checks to decipher that text.
name: Unravel Mysteries
prerequisite_markdown: master in a [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=22) skill; [Scrollmaster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1130)

**Access**
A character who is a member of the Pathfinder Society has access to this option.

</column>

---

As a Spellmaster, you are adept at identifying magic. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus when you [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=24) with a skill in which you are trained or better. If you have the [Wayfinder Resonance Tinkerer](/Feats.aspx?ID=875) feat, you can change the cantrip you chose for that feat each day during your daily preparations.

**Special** You cannot select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the Spellmaster archetype.

<title level="2">Spellmaster Dedication leads to...</title>
[Absorb Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=1138), [Communal Sustain](/Feats.aspx?ID=2245), [Familiar Form](/Feats.aspx?ID=2241), [Glyph Expert](/Feats.aspx?ID=2242), [Master's Counterspell](/Feats.aspx?ID=2247), [Mobile Magical Combat](/Feats.aspx?ID=2248), [Recover Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=2243), [Spellmaster's Resilience](/Feats.aspx?ID=1137), [Spellmaster's Ward](/Feats.aspx?ID=2244), [Surreptitious Spellcaster](/Feats.aspx?ID=1135), [Ward Casting](/Feats.aspx?ID=1136)
name: Spellmaster Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: Intelligence 14, Wisdom 14, or Charisma 14; ability to cast focus spells; member of the Pathfinder Society affiliated with the School of Spells

You carefully ward your spell by drawing from a mantle of magical energy you prepared to protect your casting, increasing your chance of retaining the spell. You reduce the DC of the flat check for Steady Spellcasting from 15 to 10.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

[Spellmaster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1134)

</column>

---

Your experience with a specific type of harmful magic makes you more resistant to it. Choose a magical tradition (arcane, divine, occult, or primal). You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against spells and effects with that tradition’s trait and resistance 5 to all damage from spells and effects with that tradition’s trait. The effect must have actually been cast or created with that tradition, rather than simply being on that tradition’s spell list.

<title level="2">Spellmaster's Resilience leads to...</title>
[Spellmaster's Tenacity](/Feats.aspx?ID=2246)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Absorb Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=1138) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 114

**Archetype** [Spellmaster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=32)

**Frequency**
once per 10 minutes

**Prerequisites**
[Spellmaster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1134); spell repertoire or ability to prepare spells

**Trigger**
You critically succeed at a saving throw against a foe’s spell, and that spell is of a level you can normally cast.

</column>

---

You absorb a spell and store it in your body. If you are a spontaneous spellcaster, you can cast the triggering spell once during the next 10 minutes, even if it’s not on your spell list as if it were in your repertoire. If you are a prepared spellcaster, you can replace one of your prepared spells of the same level with the absorbed spell, but you must cast the absorbed spell within 10 minutes or you lose that spell slot for the day. You cast the spell at the same spell level as the spell you absorbed, but the spell is of your magical tradition and uses your spell DC, spell attack roll, and other statistics to determine its effects.

<title level="2">Absorb Spell leads to...</title>
[Retain Absorbed Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=2249)
name: Absorb Spell
prerequisite_markdown: [Spellmaster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1134); spell repertoire or ability to prepare spells

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Swordmaster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1139) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 115

**Archetype** [Swordmaster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=33)

**Prerequisites**
Strength 14, Dexterity 14, or Constitution 14; member of the Pathfinder Society affiliated with the School of Swords

**Access**
A character who is a member of the Pathfinder Society has access to this option.

</column>

---

Your Swords training taught you to never lose hold of your weapon. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your Reflex DC when foes attempt to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) you. If you have the [Deft Cooperation](/Feats.aspx?ID=873) feat and critically succeed on a check to [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=75) an ally’s attack roll or skill check, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus from Deft Cooperation (instead of a +1 bonus) the first time you attempt an attack roll or attempt a skill check where the bonus would apply.

**Special** You cannot select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the Swordmaster archetype.

<title level="2">Swordmaster Dedication leads to...</title>
[Armored Exercise](/Feats.aspx?ID=2262), [Armored Rest](/Feats.aspx?ID=2255), [Death's Door](/Feats.aspx?ID=1142), [Emergency Medical Assistance](/Feats.aspx?ID=2258), [Focus Ally](/Feats.aspx?ID=2264), [Forceful Shot](/Feats.aspx?ID=2263), [Harrying Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=1140), [Improvised Crafting](/Feats.aspx?ID=2250), [Martial Exercise](/Feats.aspx?ID=2256), [Physical Training](/Feats.aspx?ID=2251), [Practiced Defender](/Feats.aspx?ID=2257), [Protect Ally](/Feats.aspx?ID=2252), [Quick Stow (Swordmaster)](/Feats.aspx?ID=2253), [Recollect Studies](/Feats.aspx?ID=2259), [Reflexive Grip](/Feats.aspx?ID=2254), [Rugged Survivalist](/Feats.aspx?ID=2260), [Shoulder Catastrophe](/Feats.aspx?ID=1141), [Tense Negotiator](/Feats.aspx?ID=2261)
name: Swordmaster Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: Strength 14, Dexterity 14, or Constitution 14; member of the Pathfinder Society affiliated with the School of Swords

</traits>

[Swordmaster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1139)

**Trigger**
An enemy critically succeeds at a Strike against an adjacent ally.

</column>

---

You’ve trained to protect your allies by diverting the worst attacks to yourself. The enemy doesn’t double the damage from the triggering Strike but instead applies the normal damage to both you and your ally. Both of you take any effects that would happen on a hit that dealt damage, such as poison, but neither of you are subject to any effects that would happen only on a critical success.

<title level="2">Shoulder Catastrophe leads to...</title>
[Stave Off Catastrophe](/Feats.aspx?ID=2265)

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Death's Door](/Feats.aspx?ID=1142) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Frequency**
once per 10 minutes

**Prerequisites**
[Swordmaster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1139)

**Trigger**
You take damage that would reduce you to 0 Hit Points.

</column>

---

You continue to fight when others would fall. Just before taking the triggering damage, you gain temporary Hit Points equal to twice your level. If you have Hit Points remaining after gaining these temporary Hit Points, you aren’t knocked unconscious, nor do you suffer the other effects of being reduced to 0 Hit Points. Any temporary Hit Points that remain after applying the damage last for up to 4 rounds.
name: Death's Door

    pfs=""
>
[False Faith](/Feats.aspx?ID=1144) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=26" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #148: Fires of the Haunted City](/Sources.aspx?ID=13) pg. 63

**Prerequisites**
worshipper of [Droskar](/Deities.aspx?ID=28)

</column>

---

Droskar knows you may need to disguise yourself in societies that oppose your faith. After spending 1 hour in prayer to Droskar, you can cast spells using the religious symbol of a different deity as your divine focus, and you can address verbal prayers to that deity. Droskar intercepts your prayers and answers them without the named god knowing.

 This ability applies to the religious symbol of only one god at a time—making a different religious symbol work in this fashion requires another hour of prayer and causes the old one to cease functioning. You can always use a religious symbol of Droskar without affecting this ability.
name: False Faith

    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Crystal Keeper Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1146) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Age of Ashes</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #148: Fires of the Haunted City](/Sources.aspx?ID=13) pg. 74

**Archetype** [Crystal Keeper](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=34)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Elven Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) or Trained in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)

</column>

---

You can use Arcana, Occultism, Religion, or Society to Decipher Writing by meditating before a crystal, regardless of the type of writing. When you Decipher Writing and roll a critical failure, you get a failure instead, and when you Decipher Writing and roll a success, you get a critical success instead.

 Additionally, you gain resistance 10 to damage from hazards associated with crystals.

**Special** You can’t select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the crystal keeper archetype.

<title level="2">Crystal Keeper Dedication leads to...</title>
[Armor Rune Shifter](/Feats.aspx?ID=1149), [Crystal Ward Spells](/Feats.aspx?ID=1147), [Simple Crystal Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=1148), [Weapon-Rune Shifter](/Feats.aspx?ID=1150)

prerequisite_markdown: Trained in [Elven Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) or Trained in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Crystal Ward Spells](/Feats.aspx?ID=1147) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #148: Fires of the Haunted City](/Sources.aspx?ID=13) pg. 74

**Archetype** [Crystal Keeper](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=34)

**Prerequisites**
[Crystal Keeper Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1146)

</column>

---

Your study of crystals and the energies held within a gem’s facets grants you special arcane crystal ward spells, which are a type of focus spell. It costs 1 Focus Point to cast a focus spell, and you start with a focus pool of 1 Focus Point. You refill your focus pool when you make your next daily preparations, and you can regain 1 Focus Point by spending 10 minutes using the Refocus activity to research and memorize the properties of gems.

 Focus spells are automatically heightened to half your level rounded up. Certain feats can give you more focus spells and increase the size of your focus pool, though your focus pool can never hold more than 3 Focus Points. You gain the [_electrified crystal ward_](/Spells.aspx?ID=561) focus spell, and you are trained in arcane spell attack rolls and spell DCs. Your spellcasting ability is Intelligence.
name: Crystal Ward Spells

<traits>

    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Simple Crystal Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=1148) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Prerequisites**
[Crystal Keeper Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1146)

</column>

---

As long as you meditate before a gemstone worth at least 10 gp as part of your daily preparations, you are capable of seeing magic as flowing runes that reveal information about magic objects, and you can scribe a personal rune that represents you. You gain the [_read aura_](/Spells.aspx?ID=246) and [_sigil_](/Spells.aspx?ID=286) cantrips. You cast these cantrips as arcane innate spells at will. A cantrip is heightened to a spell level equal to half your level rounded up.
name: Simple Crystal Magic

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Armor Rune Shifter](/Feats.aspx?ID=1149) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #148: Fires of the Haunted City](/Sources.aspx?ID=13) pg. 75

**Archetype** [Crystal Keeper](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=34)

**Prerequisites**
[Crystal Keeper Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1146)

</column>

---

You learn the secrets of armor runes by viewing them through gems and can alter them at your discretion. Once per day when you make your daily preparations, you can focus on a rune’s magical energies through a crystal’s facets to alter a single property rune on one suit of armor in your possession to suppress its normal effects and make it function as a different property rune. You can temporarily alter the rune to function as one of the following runes: [_energy-resistant_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=283), [_fortification_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=285), [_glamered_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=286), [_invisibility_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=287), [_shadow_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=288), or [_slick_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=289). The property rune remains altered until you make your next daily preparations. The armor must meet the prerequisites for the temporary rune, and any other runes that required the suppressed rune are also suppressed while the rune is transformed. The rune is obviously altered, and thus the armor can’t be sold as though it had the temporary rune.
name: Armor Rune Shifter

You learn how to alter the magic of weapon runes by using a crystal as a focusing component. Once per day during your daily preparations, you can focus on a rune’s magical energies through a crystal’s facets to alter a single property rune on one weapon in your possession to suppress its normal effects and make it function as a different property rune. You can temporarily alter the rune to function as one of the following runes: [_corrosive_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=292), [_flaming_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295), [_frost_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=296), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), [_grievous_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=298), [_returning_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=301), [_shock_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=303), [_thundering_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=306), or [_wounding_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=309). The property rune remains altered until you make your next daily preparations. The weapon must meet the prerequisites for the temporary rune, and any other runes that required the suppressed rune are also suppressed while the rune is transformed. The rune is obviously altered, and thus the weapon can’t be sold as though it had the temporary rune.

    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Deadly Poison Weapon](/Feats.aspx?ID=1151) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Age of Ashes</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Rogue" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=139" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #149: Against the Scarlet Triad](/Sources.aspx?ID=14) pg. 78

**Prerequisites**
[Improved Poison Weapon](/Feats.aspx?ID=573)

</column>

---

Your poisons are devastatingly potent. When you apply a simple poison with [Poison Weapon](/Feats.aspx?ID=563), the poison deals 4d4 poison damage instead of 1d4 poison damage. When you apply an [injury](/Traits.aspx?ID=97) poison to your weapon, the DC to resist that poison increases by 2, to a maximum of your class DC.

<title level="2">Katapesh Poisoners</title>

**Source** [Pathfinder #149: Against the Scarlet Triad](/Sources.aspx?ID=14) pg. 78

Famous not only for its pesh cash crop and the host of medicinal plants that grow naturally in the nearby desert, but also for the consistent demand for efficient toxins, Katapesh has developed its own specialized poisons and toxic techniques. The city's infamous Poison Maker's Guild is the source of many of these concoctions as well as secret techniques for using them, though unscrupulous Katapeshi bodyguards, thieves, and assassins all know at least one poison recipe or a unique means of inflicting such toxins.

**Related Feats**: [Pesh Skin](/Feats.aspx?ID=1152), [Shaped Contaminant](/Feats.aspx?ID=1153)
name: Deadly Poison Weapon

    pfs="Limited"
>
[Pesh Skin](/Feats.aspx?ID=1152) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Age of Ashes</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Druid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=52" />
<trait label="Morph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=113" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #149: Against the Scarlet Triad](/Sources.aspx?ID=14) pg. 78

**Requirements**
You are transformed into a plant by a polymorph spell.

</column>

---

Your plant form sprouts hundreds of spines, and your blood causes mild hallucinations in creatures exposed to it. Each time a creature touches you, hits you with an unarmed attack, or hits you with a melee weapon attack while adjacent to you, that creature takes 1d6 piercing damage. Increase the damage to 2d6 if the polymorph spell is 8th level or higher, or to 3d6 if it’s 10th level.

 When an adjacent creature damages you with piercing or slashing damage, it must succeed at a Fortitude save against your class DC or become [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (or stupefied 2 on a critical failure) until the end of its next turn.

 These benefits last until you’re no longer polymorphed into a plant.

<title level="2">Katapesh Poisoners</title>

**Source** [Pathfinder #149: Against the Scarlet Triad](/Sources.aspx?ID=14) pg. 78

Famous not only for its pesh cash crop and the host of medicinal plants that grow naturally in the nearby desert, but also for the consistent demand for efficient toxins, Katapesh has developed its own specialized poisons and toxic techniques. The city's infamous Poison Maker's Guild is the source of many of these concoctions as well as secret techniques for using them, though unscrupulous Katapeshi bodyguards, thieves, and assassins all know at least one poison recipe or a unique means of inflicting such toxins.

**Related Feats**: [Deadly Poison Weapon](/Feats.aspx?ID=1151), [Shaped Contaminant](/Feats.aspx?ID=1153)
name: Pesh Skin

Through a combination of careful manipulation and precise breath control, you can deploy inhaled toxins precisely. When you activate an [inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=96) poison, you can cause it to fill a 20-foot line that’s 5 feet tall rather than a 10-foot cube. You gain a +3 status bonus to saving throws against inhaled poisons that you activate.

**Source** [Pathfinder #149: Against the Scarlet Triad](/Sources.aspx?ID=14) pg. 78

Famous not only for its pesh cash crop and the host of medicinal plants that grow naturally in the nearby desert, but also for the consistent demand for efficient toxins, Katapesh has developed its own specialized poisons and toxic techniques. The city's infamous Poison Maker's Guild is the source of many of these concoctions as well as secret techniques for using them, though unscrupulous Katapeshi bodyguards, thieves, and assassins all know at least one poison recipe or a unique means of inflicting such toxins.

**Related Feats**: [Deadly Poison Weapon](/Feats.aspx?ID=1151), [Pesh Skin](/Feats.aspx?ID=1152)

</column>

---

As a Zephyr Guard, you’re always vigilant against crime and threats to the city’s safety. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Perception checks against attempts to [Palm an Object](/Actions.aspx?ID=67), [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=68), or [Conceal an Object](/Actions.aspx?ID=61) (including you’re [Seeking](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) concealed objects). You become trained in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) and Katapesh [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8); if you were already trained, you become an expert instead.

**Special** You can’t select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the Zephyr Guard archetype.

<title level="2">Zephyr Guard Dedication leads to...</title>
[Decry Thief](/Feats.aspx?ID=1155), [Know the Beat](/Feats.aspx?ID=1156), [Relentless Disarm](/Feats.aspx?ID=1157), [Unfazed Assessment](/Feats.aspx?ID=1158)

<title level="2">Paragons of Promise</title>

**Source** [Pathfinder #150: Broken Promises](/Sources.aspx?ID=15) pg. 75

The citizens of Promise represent some of the most capable experts, greatest minds, and powerful warriors on Golarion—ideal teachers and trainers for PCs who want to maximize their own potential. If the PCs secure Visitor’s Pins or an alliance with Mengkare, their access to Promise’s trainers and records unlock all of the following uncommon class feats.

**Related Feats**: [Aura of Unbreakable Virtue](/Feats.aspx?ID=1161), [Bloodline Metamorphosis](/Feats.aspx?ID=1162), [Efficient Alchemy (Paragon)](/Feats.aspx?ID=1163), [Emblazon Divinity](/Feats.aspx?ID=1164), [Enduring Debilitation](/Feats.aspx?ID=1165), [Golden Body](/Feats.aspx?ID=1166), [Song of the Fallen](/Feats.aspx?ID=1168), [Spell Mastery](/Feats.aspx?ID=1843), [Superior Sight](/Feats.aspx?ID=1170), [Ultimate Flexibility](/Feats.aspx?ID=1732), [Unstoppable Juggernaut](/Feats.aspx?ID=1631)

You can choose to suppress or resume this aura as an action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait.

Your sacred etchings imbue objects with power. It takes you only 1 minute to emblazon a symbol using [Emblazon Armament](/Feats.aspx?ID=272), and you can have up to four symbols emblazoned at a time. Each item can still have only one symbol emblazoned upon it, and if you exceed the limit of four, the oldest symbol disappears. These symbols can benefit even those who don’t follow the deity, provided they aren’t directly opposed (as determined by the GM).

 You can select a different benefit for each emblazoned symbol, chosen from any you have from Emblazon Armament or other feats such as [Emblazon Energy](/Feats.aspx?ID=289) or [Emblazon Antimagic](/Feats.aspx?ID=296).

You are unstoppable, able to shrug off mortal wounds with ease. You gain resistance equal to 3 + your Constitution modifier to all damage, and your resistance from raging resistance increases to 8 + your Constitution modifier.

Your experience keeps you on your toes, allowing you to adapt to even the most dangerous challenges. When you gain a fighter feat using combat flexibility, you gain three fighter feats instead of one. The first feat must still be 8th level or lower, the second feat can be up to 14th level, and the third feat can be up to 18th level. You can use the first feat to meet the prerequisites of the second or third feats and the second feat to meet the prerequisites of the third feat. You must meet all feats’ prerequisites. In addition, you can adapt to the battlefield’s challenges by spending 1 hour to train. If you do, you can reselect the feats chosen with combat flexibility as if you had made your daily preparations.
name: Ultimate Flexibility

[Splinter Faith](/Feats.aspx?ID=1173) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Champion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=24" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=26" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gods & Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=17) pg. 8

</column>

---

Your faith in your deity is represented in an extremely unusual way that some might call heretical. When you select this feat, you should detail the fundamental tenets of your splinter faith, though it shouldn’t affect the deity’s edicts or anathema. Choose four domains. These domains must be chosen from among your deity’s domains, your deity’s alternate domains, and up to one domain that isn’t on either list and isn’t anathematic to your deity. Any domain spell you cast from a domain that isn’t on either of your deity’s lists is always heightened to 1 level lower than usual for a focus spell. For the purpose of abilities that depend on your deity’s domains, the four domains you chose are your deity’s domains, and any of your deity’s domains you didn’t choose are now among your deity’s alternate domains.

**Special** Unless you take this feat at 1st level, changing the way you relate to your deity requires retraining, as described in [Changing Faith](/Rules.aspx?ID=803). If you take this feat and previously benefited from any effect that requires a domain your splinter faith doesn’t include, such as a domain spell from [Domain Initiate](/Feats.aspx?ID=265), you lose that effect.
name: Splinter Faith

markdown:
**Source** [Gods & Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=17) pg. 104 **Prerequisites** Master in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13); you follow a deity --- Calling out to your deity, you recite scripture to harm a foe. When you select this feat, choose [chaos](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83), or [law](/Traits.aspx?ID=99). Your choice must match one of your deity’s alignment components. This action has the trait corresponding to the chosen alignment. Attempt a [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) check against the Will DC of a foe within 30 feet. The foe is then temporarily immune to Battle Prayers from your deity for 1 day. **Critical Success** You deal 2d6 damage of the chosen alignment type, or 6d6 damage if you have legendary proficiency in Religion.
**Success** You deal 1d6 damage of the chosen alignment type, or 3d6 damage if you have legendary proficiency in Religion.
**Failure** There is no effect.
**Critical Failure** The backlash of your foe’s will against your prayer prevents you from using Battle Prayer again for 10 minutes.
name: Battle Prayer
prerequisite_markdown: Master in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13); you follow a deity

    right="Feat 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bless Tonic](/Feats.aspx?ID=1175) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
<trait label="Positive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=128" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
