Master in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4); deity who grants [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) divine font

</column>

---

You channel positive energy into restoratives that ward against harm. When you [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) an alchemical item that restores Hit Points (rather than create it with advanced alchemy, Quick Alchemy, or another means), it becomes a blessed alchemical item and gains the [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) trait. For example, an [elixir of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=91) would become a blessed elixir of life. When a creature regains Hit Points using a blessed alchemical item, the overflowing energy grants them negative resistance 5 for 1 round, or negative resistance 10 if you are legendary in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4).
name: Bless Tonic
prerequisite_markdown: Master in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4); deity who grants [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) divine font

</column>

---

You rapidly adapt to environments through a combination of skill, meditation, and divine grace. After spending an hour in an environment of [severe cold or colder](/Rules.aspx?ID=642), you are protected from the effects of severe cold until you leave that environment. After spending an hour in an environment of severe heat or hotter, you are protected from the effects of severe heat until you leave that environment.

**Source** [Gods & Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=17) pg. 105 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) For the purposes of Society play, remove the final clause of Evangelize that states “at the GM’s discretion, a target that genuinely changes its perspective to support your faith as a result of the argument is also otherwise unaffected.”_ **Archetype** [Steel Falcon](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=374) (Level 7) **Prerequisites** Master in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6); you follow a deity or philosophy --- You point out a detail that incontrovertibly supports your faith, causing a listener’s mind to whirl. Attempt a Diplomacy check and compare the result to the Will DC of a single target that can hear you and understands your language; that target is then temporarily immune to Evangelize with respect to your deity or philosophy for 1 day. A creature that already agrees with you is unaffected, and at the GM’s discretion, a target that genuinely changes its perspective to support your faith as a result of the argument is also otherwise unaffected. **Critical Success** The target is [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 round.
**Success** The target is stupefied 1 for 1 round.
**Failure** The target is unaffected.
name: Evangelize
prerequisite_markdown: Master in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6); you follow a deity or philosophy

If you give the container to another character for them to throw, they use their proficiency with improvised weapons instead._

**Prerequisites**
Master in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13); good or evil deity

</column>

---

You imbue water with your deity’s blessing. Choose a container of water on your person that is of light Bulk. For 1 round, if you or an ally throw this container at a foe, it has the damaging effects of [holy water](/Equipment.aspx?ID=245) (if you follow a good deity) or [unholy water](/Equipment.aspx?ID=248) (if you follow an evil deity). The water’s transient power doesn’t make it gain any value, allow it to be used to supply the cost for a spell or ritual, and so on. If you have legendary proficiency in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13), you can sanctify three containers held by you or allies within your reach instead of one.
name: Sanctify Water

<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=26" />
</traits>

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Remove the final sentence of Syncretism that specifies the feat’s benefit if your cleric doctrine is not cloistered cleric or warpriest._

**Prerequisites**
you follow a deity

</column>

---

You have come to see the overlap between two deities’ teachings. Choose a second deity. You must meet their alignment requirements, and you are subject to their edicts and anathema. If you are a cloistered cleric, select one of that deity’s domains, gaining the benefits of the [Expanded Domain Initiate](/Feats.aspx?ID=1172) feat with that domain. If you are a warpriest, you gain the favored weapon of that deity as a second favored weapon, and it gains the benefits of feats and abilities you have that affect your deity’s favored weapon, such as [Deadly Simplicity](/Feats.aspx?ID=264). If you have a different doctrine than cloistered cleric or warpriest, either apply whichever of the above options is most appropriate for your doctrine, or, at the GM’s discretion, add a benefit from the second deity more closely tied to your doctrine.
name: Syncretism

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Reflexive Catch](/Feats.aspx?ID=1190) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #151: The Show Must Go On](/Sources.aspx?ID=21) pg. 74

**Archetype** [Juggler](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=36)

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1); [Juggler Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1187)

**Trigger**
An item falls within your reach or an attacker fails an attack roll to hit you or a creature within your reach with a thrown weapon.

**Requirements**
The item or thrown weapon is of a Bulk you can [Juggle](/Feats.aspx?ID=1199). If the trigger was a thrown weapon attack targeting you, you can’t be [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to the attack. If you were already Juggling, you can’t be Juggling your maximum number of items.

</column>

---

You grab the triggering item. If you are Juggling, you can immediately add the item to the objects you’re Juggling as a free action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) and [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) traits.
name: Reflexive Catch
requirement_markdown: The item or thrown weapon is of a Bulk you can [Juggle](/Feats.aspx?ID=1199). If the trigger was a thrown weapon attack targeting you, you can’t be [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to the attack. If you were already Juggling, you can’t be Juggling your maximum number of items.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 16"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Opportune Throw](/Feats.aspx?ID=1192) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #151: The Show Must Go On](/Sources.aspx?ID=21) pg. 74

**Archetype** [Juggler](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=36)

**Prerequisites**
Legendary in [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12); [Juggler Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1187)

**Trigger**
A creature within 10 feet of you uses a [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) action or a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) action, makes a ranged attack, or leaves a square during a move action it’s using.

**Requirements**
You are Juggling a [thrown](/Traits.aspx?ID=195) weapon.

</column>

---

You make a thrown ranged Strike with the required weapon against the triggering creature. If you critically succeed and the trigger was a manipulate action, you [disrupt](/Rules.aspx?ID=394) that action. This Strike doesn’t count against your multiple attack penalty, and your multiple attack penalty doesn’t apply to this Strike.
name: Opportune Throw
requirement_markdown: You are Juggling a [thrown](/Traits.aspx?ID=195) weapon.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Staff Acrobat Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1193) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #151: The Show Must Go On](/Sources.aspx?ID=21) pg. 75

**Archetype** [Staff Acrobat](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=37)

**Prerequisites**
Dexterity 16; Trained in [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1); Trained in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3); trained with at least one of the following weapons: [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=12), [bo staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=17), [halfling sling staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=78), or any weapon in the [spear](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=14) or [polearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=11) group (referred to in this archetype as “your staff”)

</column>

---

When you [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=36), [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=81), or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=37) while wielding your staff, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to any [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check required and add 5 feet to the distance you can Leap vertically and horizontally. You can [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) and [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) even if you don’t have a free hand, provided you are wielding your staff. Finally, when you roll a success on a check to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=28) while wielding your staff, you get a critical success instead.

**Special** You cannot select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the staff acrobat archetype.

<title level="2">Staff Acrobat Dedication leads to...</title>
[Bullying Staff](/Feats.aspx?ID=1194), [Levering Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=1196), [Pivot Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=1198), [Staff Sweep](/Feats.aspx?ID=1195), [Whirlwind Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=1197)
name: Staff Acrobat Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: Dexterity 16; Trained in [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1); Trained in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3); trained with at least one of the following weapons: [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=12), [bo staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=17), [halfling sling staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=78), or any weapon in the [spear](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=14) or [polearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=11) group (referred to in this archetype as “your staff”)

[Juggle](/Feats.aspx?ID=1199) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #151: The Show Must Go On](/Sources.aspx?ID=21) pg. 76

**Prerequisites**
Dexterity 16; Trained in [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12)

</column>

---

You can Juggle items and still use your hands between tosses. You can use the Juggle action in several ways.

 You can start to Juggle with a single stored item, an item you were carrying, or an unattended item within your reach. The item must be of light or negligible Bulk. You Juggle the item until the end of your next turn, unless you use this feat against next turn to continue Juggling (see below). While you are Juggling, you can use this action again to add another item to the Juggle, which must meet all the same requirements. You can Juggle a number of items equal to one more than the number of hands you are using to Juggle.

 You can also use this action to continue Juggling as long as you aren’t fatigued. When you do, you continue to Juggle until the end of your next turn. If you do not continue Juggling by the end of your turn, you cease Juggling and you drop all the items. You become fatigued if you continue Juggling for more than 10 minutes (60 rounds) within the course of an hour.

 As long as you’re Juggling fewer than your maximum number of items, you have a free hand. You can wield weapons you are Juggling (but not shields or other items) as long as they can be wielded in one hand. For example, while you Juggle a dagger in one hand and wield a shield in the other, you are wielding the dagger but still have a free hand to Cast a Spell with a material component. Juggling two or more weapons with one hand doesn’t allow you to use feats that require two weapons each held in a different hand.
name: Juggle

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 201 **Prerequisites** Trained in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) **Requirements** The target ally lost Stamina Points within the last round. --- You give an ally within 30 feet a quick pep talk, helping them recover. Attempt a Diplomacy check. The DC is usually 15, though the GM might adjust it based on the circumstances. If you have expert proficiency in Diplomacy, you can instead attempt a DC 20 check to increase the Stamina Points recovered by 5; if you have master proficiency, you can attempt a DC 30 check to increase the Stamina Points by 15; if you have legendary proficiency, you can attempt a DC 40 check to increase the Stamina Points by 25. No matter the result, the ally is temporarily immune to Encouraging Words until they either [Take a Breather](/Actions.aspx?ID=463) or rest for the day. **Critical Success** The ally recovers 2d8 Stamina Points.
**Success** The ally recovers 1d8 Stamina Points.
**Critical Failure** The ally takes 1d8 mental damage, but this can reduce only Stamina Points, never Hit Points.
name: Encouraging Words
requirement_markdown: The target ally lost Stamina Points within the last round.

---

Your hands work as effectively as shovels when you need to dig a quick hole. You dig a shallow pit in the square you currently occupy, turning the square into difficult terrain (including for you). In addition, you also kick up a cloud of grit in a direction of your choice. The cloud extinguishes small, unattended, non-magical fires and helps smother the flames on burning creatures located within the cone; creatures in this cone that are taking [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) can immediately attempt an additional flat DC 15 check to end the persistent damage.

You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks to [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=29) the spaces of Medium or larger enemies, and the presence of an enemy doesn’t make the squares difficult terrain.

Growing up in shoony society, you learned the value of honesty, friendship, and hard work, even during trying times. You gain the trained proficiency rank in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) and [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16). If you would automatically become trained in one of those skills (from your background or class, for example), you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You also become trained in [Shoony Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8).
name: Shoony Lore

Your flesh has been specially treated with the same arcane and alchemical processes used to toughen the skin of [flesh golems](/Monsters.aspx?ID=238). Increase your maximum Hit Points by an amount equal to your level. You gain resistance to physical damage (except adamantine) equal to your number of class feats from the golem grafter archetype.

**Special** You cannot select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the golem grafter archetype.

<title level="2">Golem Grafter Dedication leads to...</title>

**Access**
You must befriend at least one Turpin Rowe logger.

</column>

---

Your logging training has made you intimately familiar with all aspects of forestry, and you have trained extensively to reach around trees and utilize their bulk to your advantage. You become trained in [Milling Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) and Forest Lore, or an expert if you were already trained. You can always [Take Cover](/Actions.aspx?ID=90) when you are within forest terrain to gain cover, even if you’re not next to an obstacle you can Take Cover behind. In addition, any enemy who gains standard cover from a tree gains only lesser cover against your attacks (or only standard cover, if the enemy otherwise gains greater cover).

**Special** You cannot select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the Turpin Rowe lumberjack archetype.

<title level="2">Turpin Rowe Lumberjack Dedication leads to...</title>
[Axe Climber](/Feats.aspx?ID=1233), [Axe Thrower](/Feats.aspx?ID=1235), [Log Roll](/Feats.aspx?ID=1234), [Widen the Gap](/Feats.aspx?ID=1236)

    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Axe Thrower](/Feats.aspx?ID=1235) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #153: Life's Long Shadows](/Sources.aspx?ID=29) pg. 75

**Archetype** [Turpin Rowe Lumberjack](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=40)

**Prerequisites**
[Turpin Rowe Lumberjack Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1232)

</column>

---

You have mastered the weight and balance of a variety of axes and can lob them with ease. Any one-handed axe weapon you wield has the [thrown](/Traits.aspx?ID=195) trait with a range of 10 feet. For axes that already have the thrown trait, the range increases by 10 feet. When you critically succeed at an attack roll with a thrown axe weapon, you apply the axe’s critical specialization effect.
name: Axe Thrower

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Widen the Gap](/Feats.aspx?ID=1236) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #153: Life's Long Shadows](/Sources.aspx?ID=29) pg. 75

**Archetype** [Turpin Rowe Lumberjack](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=40)

**Prerequisites**
[Turpin Rowe Lumberjack Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1232)

</column>

---

Once you have penetrated a foe’s defenses, you can home in on the same spot to exploit your opponent’s wound and deal some serious damage. The second time you hit and deal damage with a melee axe Strike to the same opponent or object during your turn, you ignore half of the opponent’s or object’s Hardness, if any. If you hit and deal damage with a melee axe Strike to the same opponent or object a third or fourth time during your turn, you ignore all of its Hardness, if any.
name: Widen the Gap

markdown:
**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 126, [PFS Scenario #1-17: The Perennial Crown Part 2, The Thorned Monarch](/Sources.aspx?ID=34) pg. 30 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters who have gained access to the Fey Influence feat from a Pathfinder Society scenario gain access to the expanded version of this feat printed in Lost Omens: Impossible Lands. This does not grant access to any additional feats._ --- You have been exposed to powerful fey magic. You become trained in primal DCs and spell attack rolls. You gain the [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69) trait and one of the following features that grants an innate primal spell that can be used once per day.
  • **Anteater** You can launch your tongue forward as a deadly attack, gaining [_grim tendrils_](/Spells.aspx?ID=141).
  • **[Cat Sith](/Monsters.aspx?ID=517)** You have catlike features and can appear as a mundane cat with [_pest form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=217) (cat only).
  • **Cursed Bluebird** You have blue feathers or wings. It's terrible luck to harm a bluebird, and you can manifest that power as [_ill omen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=696).
  • **[Dryad](/Monsters.aspx?ID=312)** Your body is covered in elegant vines, granting you [_summon plant or fungus_](/Spells.aspx?ID=325).
  • **Faun** Your legs and feet are swift and possibly hooved, granting you [_fleet step_](/Spells.aspx?ID=122).
  • **[Gremlin](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=58)** You have long, bat-like ears and gain [_bane_](/Spells.aspx?ID=18).
  • **Monarch** You have vestigial, insectile features and gain [_spider sting_](/Spells.aspx?ID=300).
  • **[Unicorn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=397)** You have a magical horn or a prominent symbol of one on your forehead, which you can use to cast [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148).
**Special** This feat gains the trait appropriate for your ancestry ([human](/Traits.aspx?ID=90) for [human](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=6), [goblin](/Traits.aspx?ID=81) for [goblin](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=4), etc.). Fey Influence leads to... [Cannibalize Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=4049), [Drain Emotion](/Feats.aspx?ID=4047), [Eldritch Calm](/Feats.aspx?ID=4051), [Fey Ascension](/Feats.aspx?ID=4048), [Fey Transcendence](/Feats.aspx?ID=4052), [Glamour](/Feats.aspx?ID=4050)
name: Fey Influence

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Annihilating Swing](/Feats.aspx?ID=1240) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Barbarian" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=18" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #156: The Apocalypse Prophet](/Sources.aspx?ID=36) pg. 72

</column>

---

You have learned from the brute destructive power of [xulgaths](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=102) and their kind. You make a melee Strike that ignores any resistances the target has. If you target a solid unattended object or surface with your Strike, you might automatically destroy it without an attack roll. If you target any object or surface with Hardness 20 or less that isn't a magic item or the effect of a spell, you destroy it. If the target object or surface is a magic item or the effect of a spell, you attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) it using your attack bonus with the Strike for the counteract check. On a successful counteract check, you destroy the object or surface unless it has Hardness greater than 20, is an artifact, or is similarly difficult to destroy. You destroy up to a 5-foot cube of an object or surface larger than Medium.

<title level="2">Capstone Feats</title>

**Source** [Pathfinder #156: The Apocalypse Prophet](/Sources.aspx?ID=36) pg. 72

Characters who have reached 20th level can choose from the following feats, representing the pinnacle of mortal power influenced by their triumphs.

**Related Feats**: [Astonishing Explosion](/Feats.aspx?ID=1241), [Banishing Blow](/Feats.aspx?ID=1242), [Denier of Destruction](/Feats.aspx?ID=1243), [Endurance of the Rooted Tree](/Feats.aspx?ID=1244), [Mimic Protections](/Feats.aspx?ID=1245), [Reclaim Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=1246), [Reflecting Riposte](/Feats.aspx?ID=1247), [Ringmaster's Introduction](/Feats.aspx?ID=1248), [Steal Essence](/Feats.aspx?ID=1249), [Tenacious Blood Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=1250), [Verdant Presence](/Feats.aspx?ID=1251)
name: Annihilating Swing

once per minute

**Trigger**
You use [Quick Alchemy](/Actions.aspx?ID=2) to craft an [alchemical bomb](/Equipment.aspx?Category=6&amp;Subcategory=7) that deals [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage and is at least 2 levels lower than your advanced alchemy level.

</column>

---

Your bomb incorporates an explosive spark of wonder that leaves witnesses dumbfounded. Any target hit by your Strike with the bomb is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) by you for 1 round. On a critical success, the target is also [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36).

    right="Feat 20"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Banishing Blow](/Feats.aspx?ID=1242) <actions string="Free Action" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Champion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=24" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #156: The Apocalypse Prophet](/Sources.aspx?ID=36) pg. 72

**Prerequisites**
[Fiendsbane Oath](/Feats.aspx?ID=220)

**Trigger**
You use your champion's reaction, triggered by a [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=70) within your reach.

</column>

---

The erratic teleportation magic you experienced in the Kortos Mounts has taught you a few tricks. You attempt an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) the fiend that triggered your champion's reaction; on a success, the target can't teleport or summon creatures for 1 minute. On a critical success, the target is also [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) and can't teleport or summon creatures for 1 hour, and if you are on your home plane and the target is a fiend of level 18 or lower from another plane, it is also banished to its home plane. Your multiple attack penalty doesn't apply to this attack.

name: Banishing Blow

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Denier of Destruction](/Feats.aspx?ID=1243) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=26" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #156: The Apocalypse Prophet](/Sources.aspx?ID=36) pg. 72

**Prerequisites**
[healing font](/Classes.aspx?ID=5)

**Trigger**
Any ally within 30 feet that you can heal with your _heal_ spell would take damage from an attack or effect from a source you can observe.

**Requirements**
You have a [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) spell prepared and you aren't [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to the source of the damage.

</column>

---

You can manipulate the same energies that the _aeon orbs_ use to sustain life. You cast _heal_ on the triggering target. Instead of its normal effects, the _heal_ spell reduces the triggering damage by an amount equal to the Hit Points a 1-action _heal_ spell of that level would have recovered. As usual, if this reduces the damage to 0, it might also remove additional effects of the triggering attack, such as [injury](/Traits.aspx?ID=97) poisons.

>

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />

---

Just as the Vale of Aroden retains light and life despite inhospitable surroundings, you can sustain yourself regardless of your environment. You don't need to eat, drink, or breathe. In addition, you can cast the [_wholeness of body_](/Spells.aspx?ID=488) ki spell almost effortlessly. You can cast it as a free action that triggers at the start of your turn.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Reclaim Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=1246) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Wizard" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=166" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #156: The Apocalypse Prophet](/Sources.aspx?ID=36) pg. 72

**Prerequisites**
[Reprepare Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=661)

</column>

---

You have learned to manipulate reflections of magic, similar to the _resonant reflections_. You reclaim the magic of an ongoing spell, using it reprepare a spell with a duration. You Dismiss a Spell of 4th level or lower that you cast today from a spell slot with at least half its duration remaining, even if that spell can't normally be Dismissed. You can then spend 10 minutes to reprepare the spell in that spell slot, as [Reprepare Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=661).

<title level="2">Capstone Feats</title>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Reflecting Riposte](/Feats.aspx?ID=1247) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Fighter" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=71" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #156: The Apocalypse Prophet](/Sources.aspx?ID=36) pg. 72

**Prerequisites**
[Dueling Riposte](/Feats.aspx?ID=389), [Mirror Shield](/Feats.aspx?ID=404), or [Twin Riposte](/Feats.aspx?ID=523)

**Trigger**
An opponent casts a spell and you critically succeed at a saving throw against it.

**Requirements**
You have a shield raised or are benefiting from [Dueling Parry](/Feats.aspx?ID=367) or [Twin Parry](/Feats.aspx?ID=377).

</column>

---

You attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the triggering spell using powerful magical techniques observed in the Kortos Mounts. Use your attack roll modifier with your shield or parrying weapon for your counteract check. If you counteract the spell, it is reflected upon the caster. If the spell targeted individual creatures, it targets the caster only. If the spell targeted an area centered on a point, the new area is centered on the caster. If the spell was a cone or line, the cone or line emanates from you toward the caster. If the spell has a save DC, it uses your class DC, and if it requires a spell attack roll, use the same attack roll modifier as the counteract check.

<title level="2">Capstone Feats</title>

**Source** [Pathfinder #156: The Apocalypse Prophet](/Sources.aspx?ID=36) pg. 72

Characters who have reached 20th level can choose from the following feats, representing the pinnacle of mortal power influenced by their triumphs.

name: Reflecting Riposte
requirement_markdown: You have a shield raised or are benefiting from [Dueling Parry](/Feats.aspx?ID=367) or [Twin Parry](/Feats.aspx?ID=377).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Steal Essence](/Feats.aspx?ID=1249) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Rogue" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=139" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #156: The Apocalypse Prophet](/Sources.aspx?ID=36) pg. 73

**Prerequisites**
legendary in [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) or master in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)

**Requirements**
A magic item is within your reach.

</column>

---

Being in the presence of the _aeon orbs_ as they deteriorated has taught you how to siphon energy, and you do so with the required magic item. The DC to steal the properties of a magic item is equal to the DC for the item's level or the normal DC to [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=68) the item, whichever is higher. The targeted item then becomes temporarily immune for 24 hours. If the object was on a creature, that creature is also temporarily immune (which prevents you from attempting to Steal the Essence of another object on it).

 If you successfully steal the properties of an item, it loses its magical properties for 24 hours and you can move those properties to a non-magical object of the same general kind (such as moving a hat's properties to another piece of headgear or moving a weapon's properties to another weapon) for the same length of time. If the moved properties are [counteracted](/Rules.aspx?ID=371), they return immediately to the original item. You automatically fail to Steal the Essence of an [artifact](/Equipment.aspx?Category=45), [cursed item](/Equipment.aspx?Category=49), or similar item.

<title level="2">Capstone Feats</title>

**Source** [Pathfinder #156: The Apocalypse Prophet](/Sources.aspx?ID=36) pg. 72

name: Steal Essence

May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse **Source** [Pathfinder #156: The Apocalypse Prophet](/Sources.aspx?ID=36) pg. 73 **Frequency** once per hour **Trigger** You take damage or cast a primal spell. --- The life-giving properties of the _aeon orbs_ flourish within you. Small plants or fungi magically appear on all surfaces in a 60-foot emanation around you. This life withers after 1 day unless the environment can support it. You can choose one of the following options each time you use this feat.
  • You can cause any number of 5-foot squares in the area to be light undergrowth, which is difficult terrain and provides lesser cover.
  • You can cause a tree or giant mushroom to appear in any unoccupied location in the area; it occupies an entire 5-foot square and provides standard cover. The tree or mushroom has 5 Hardness and 50 Hit Points.

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 10

</column>

---

Growing up among catfolk has taught you the traditional values of freedom to travel, stewardship of the land, and quick reactions when your curiosity lands you in trouble. You gain the trained proficiency rank in [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) and [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16). If you would automatically become trained in one of those skills (from your background or class, for example), you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You also become trained in [Catfolk Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8).
name: Catfolk Lore

You favor weapons that you can use with quick, darting slashes like a cat's claws. You are trained with the [hatchet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=27), [kama](/Weapons.aspx?ID=54), [kukri](/Weapons.aspx?ID=56), [scimitar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=38), and [sickle](/Weapons.aspx?ID=9). In addition, you gain access to [kama](/Weapons.aspx?ID=54), [kukris](/Weapons.aspx?ID=56), and all uncommon [catfolk](/Traits.aspx?ID=211) weapons. For you, martial catfolk weapons are simple weapons and advanced catfolk weapons are martial weapons.

<title level="2">Catfolk Weapon Familiarity leads to...</title>
[Catfolk Weapon Expertise](/Feats.aspx?ID=1268), [Catfolk Weapon Rake](/Feats.aspx?ID=1257)

    level="1"
    right="Feat 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Light Paws](/Feats.aspx?ID=1260) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Catfolk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=211" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 10

</column>

---

You can balance on your toes to step carefully over obstructions. You Stride and then Step, or Step and then Stride, ignoring difficult terrain during this movement.
name: Light Paws

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Springing Leaper](/Feats.aspx?ID=1261) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Catfolk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=211" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 10

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3)

</column>

---

Your powerful legs allow you to make sudden and dramatic leaps. You can [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=81) as a 2-action activity to double the distance you can Leap vertically, or Leap as a 3-action activity to triple the distance you can Leap vertically. You don't automatically fail [Long Jumps](/Actions.aspx?ID=37) for jumping in a different direction than your Stride.
name: Springing Leaper
